panels.description: Request success rate. Normal (green) if above 99.5%, critical (red) if below 98%. Only successful completions where finished_reason='stop' are counted.
panels.title: Success Rate

panels.description: Number of currently processing (Running) + waiting requests. A sharp increase signals a traffic surge.
panels.title: Active Requests

panels.description: Scheduler efficiency. Running/(Running+Waiting). 100% means all requests are processed immediately. Below 70% indicates overload.
panels.title: System Efficiency

panels.description: P99 time to first token generation. Core indicator of perceived initial response speed. Under 1s is ideal.
panels.title: TTFT P99

panels.description: [Critical] Preemption rate. Aborting running requests due to KV Cache depletion. Investigate immediately if above 0. Consider increasing GPU memory or reducing batch size.
panels.title: Preemption Rate

panels.description: Total time from request receipt to completion. P50 covers half of requests, P99 covers 99%. Spike in P99 requires bottleneck analysis.
panels.title: E2E Request Latency

panels.description: Breakdown of Prefill (input processing) vs Decode (output generation) latency. High Prefill suggests long input prompts; high Decode suggests long output generation is the bottleneck.
panels.title: Inference Stage Breakdown

panels.description: Comparison of TTFT (Time to First Token) vs TPOT (Time Per Output Token). High TTFT indicates Prefill bottleneck; high TPOT indicates Decode bottleneck.
panels.title: TTFT vs TPOT

panels.description: Tokens processed per second. Input (Prompt) = tokens received, Output (Generation) = tokens generated. Higher throughput indicates better system efficiency.
panels.title: Token Throughput

panels.description: Ratio of input tokens to output tokens. High ratio indicates long input + short output (RAG); low ratio indicates short input + long output (generation/summarisation).
panels.title: Token I/O Ratio

panels.description: Percentage of computation saved by Prefix Cache. Higher percentage means the cache is skipping more Prefill operations, saving GPU resources. (1 - actual compute / total input) * 100
panels.title: Prefix Cache Savings

panels.description: Distribution of input (Prompt) tokens per request. Darker colours indicate higher request frequency for that length. Concentrated areas suggest optimization opportunities for those lengths.
panels.title: Request Length Heatmap (Prompt)

panels.description: Time requests spent in the queue. Queue time increases when GPUs are busy. If consistently high, consider scaling out instances.
panels.title: Request Queue Time

panels.description: GPU KV Cache utilization. Memory area storing intermediate computation results. Above 80% warrants caution; above 95% risks Preemption.
panels.title: GPU KV Cache Usage

panels.description: Prefix Cache hit rate. Rate of reusing identical system prompts or few-shot examples. Above 70% is efficient; key indicator in RAG environments.
panels.title: Prefix Cache Hit Rate

panels.description: Request count by scheduler state. Running = Inferencing on GPU, Waiting = In queue, Swapped = Swapped out of memory. Sustained increase in Waiting indicates overload.
panels.title: Scheduler State

panels.description: Python GC count and process memory usage. Frequent GC or sustained RSS growth suggests a potential memory leak.
panels.title: Python GC & Memory

panels.description: Distribution of request completion reasons. stop = normal termination (EOS), length = maximum length reached, abort = interrupted. High abort rate indicates issues.
panels.title: Finish Reason Distribution

panels.description: Trend of total request throughput vs successful requests. Any gap indicates failed requests. These should consistently match in a healthy state.
panels.title: Throughput vs Success
